title: City of Chaos - Let's Play - Part 7
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sHQg97i_Hp0

Daniel (@dungeondive) brings the City of Chaos playthrough to its (incomplete) close, adopting his fast-travel house rule to push toward a resolution. The Wizard War plot pays off best: Bing threatens the apothecary Beldon, who confesses he caused the entire chaos — a botched potion and a spiteful rumour set sorcerers Najera and Muirhead at war — and begs Bing to carry his letter of apology. Armed with the looking-glass, Bing re-enters the Marble Minaret, deflects Muirhead's lightning, presents the letter, and is handed the Orb of Absorption to place on the altar before Nestra — but with no idea where Nestra or the altar are, the thread stalls.

Dizzy's quest to learn the ring's secret is repeatedly thwarted: the Duelists' Academy demands paid training, and a roving rat pack scurries over to block the building entirely. A postal-delivery sidequest and a broken-fiddle vignette round out the wandering.

Daniel calls the run with no plot fully solved — three hours in, with other games waiting — but promises a review and a streamlined variant so he can someday see a complete narrative arc.

----

Would you have kept going to finish a story arc, or is calling it at three hours the right move when a game fights you this hard?
